summary: Canonical donut-plus-legend dashboard card with shared data-viz chrome, a filter action, fill chart region, content-sized legend, and built-in hover synchronization.
useWhen: A dashboard widget needs the standard donut and visible legend composition in one data-viz card.
Chart slices and legend rows should cross-highlight without application hover wiring.
The card needs the shared header filter action while the application owns filter state and menu content.
avoidWhen: The visualization is a bar, line, metric, or custom chart; compose CardShellDataViz directly.
The product needs a custom legend treatment such as chips, external stat callouts, or selectable filters.
The surface is a settings section or generic content card.

alternatives.when: The visualization needs shared data-viz chrome without the canonical donut layout.
alternatives.reason: CardShellDataViz owns only the header, actions, width, and body boundary.

alternatives.when: A donut and legend need a custom layout without card chrome.
alternatives.reason: Compose the chart and ChartLegend directly and own their layout and hover synchronization.

accessibility: Provide a concise card heading and a localized filter label for the icon-only header action.
Keep essential values available through the chart and visible legend rather than encoding meaning through slice color alone.
The application owns meaningful loading, empty, and error status copy when either content region is temporarily replaced or absent.
states: The chart region fills remaining card height while the legend remains content-sized.
Chart and legend regions render only when their corresponding slots contain content, allowing the owner to substitute loading, empty, or error presentations without dangling region padding.
Hover synchronization changes highlight only; it never represents selection, filtering, or navigation.
The filter action emits intent while the parent owns popup content, applied constraints, and data updates.
responsiveBehavior: Select the token-based card width appropriate to the parent grid; the matching minimum height preserves the chart region.
The parent dashboard owns wrapping and column changes while the internal chart-over-legend arrangement remains stable.

customElements: Assign chart data and legend items as JavaScript properties and place the components in the chart and legend slots.
react: Keep filter and dataset state in the owner; the card handles hover synchronization for the canonical slotted pair.
angular: Keep filter and dataset state in the owner; the card handles hover synchronization for the canonical slotted pair.
